claim: Did Meta really lay off 5% of its workforce?

sources.title: Meta to cut roughly 5% of staff in performance-based reduction
sources.snippet: Meta said it would reduce its workforce by about 5% as part of a performance review cycle.
sources.page_text: Meta Platforms said on Wednesday it would cut roughly 5% of its workforce. In an internal memo, CEO Mark Zuckerberg wrote: "We are reducing our workforce by approximately 5% across affected teams." The company employs about 74,000 people, implying a reduction of around 3,700 roles. Analysts noted the cuts were framed as performance-based rather than a broad restructuring.

sources.title: Meta Platforms Form 8-K — workforce reduction disclosure
sources.snippet: Registrant announced a reduction of approximately 5% of its global workforce.
sources.page_text: On July 16, 2026, Meta Platforms, Inc. announced a reduction of approximately 5% of its global workforce. The Company expects to incur charges in connection with this reduction. This filing constitutes the official disclosure of the action described herein.

sources.title: Leaked internal doc suggests deeper cuts than announced
sources.snippet: An internal spreadsheet reviewed by us logs 8-10% in some org charts.
sources.page_text: A document circulating internally claims the real figure is closer to 8-10% in certain product orgs. One line reads: "some teams are seeing 8-10% reductions well beyond the announced 5%." The authenticity of the document could not be independently verified and Meta declined to comment.

sources.title: Layoff tracker logs Meta reductions
sources.snippet: Community-sourced tracker logs 4,200 Meta roles affected.
sources.page_text: Our community-sourced tracker currently logs 4,200 affected Meta roles, based on self-reports. The tracker notes: "4,200 roles logged so far, self-reported and unverified." Figures typically lag official announcements.

sources.title: Meta layoffs megathread
sources.snippet: Employees and observers react to the 5% reduction announcement.
sources.page_text: Reddit megathread on the Meta layoffs.

top_comments.author: throwaway_meta
top_comments.body: Got the email this morning. "Framed as performance but my whole team is gone." Brutal.

top_comments.author: pnw_dev
top_comments.body: 5% official but recruiting and several infra teams look way more than 5% hit.

top_comments.author: analyst_anon
top_comments.body: Market reaction muted, stock up premarket. Investors read this as cost discipline.
